---
title: "Day 1: Madison to Pisa"
subtitle: "Departure Day"
date: "2026-06-04"
day-of-week: "Wednesday"
location: "Madison, WI → Pisa, Italy"
categories: [itinerary, travel, departure]
---

## Overview

**The Journey Begins!** Departure from Madison/Chicago area, overnight transatlantic flight, arriving in Pisa the morning of June 5.

**Today's Focus:** Safe travel, rest on plane, arrival preparation

---

## Pre-Departure Checklist

### Final Preparations

- [ ] Confirm flight status online
- [ ] Print or download boarding passes (24 hours before)
- [ ] Charge all electronics (phone, camera, portable battery)
- [ ] Pack carry-on with essentials for first 24 hours
- [ ] Have passport accessible but secure
- [ ] Verify credit cards work (notify banks already done)
- [ ] Empty refrigerator of perishables
- [ ] Set thermostat, lock windows/doors
- [ ] Arrange pet care or plant watering (if applicable)
- [ ] Take out trash
- [ ] Unplug unnecessary electronics

### What to Bring

**Carry-On Must-Haves:**
- Passport (check expiration!)
- Boarding passes (digital + printed backup)
- Credit cards, small amount of cash
- Phone + charger + portable battery
- Medications (full 2-week supply)
- 1-2 changes of clothes (in case checked bag delayed)
- Toiletries (3.4oz/100ml limit)
- Sweater or light jacket (planes are cold)
- Eye mask, earplugs, neck pillow
- Snacks for flight
- Entertainment (book, downloaded movies, podcasts)
- Empty water bottle (fill after security)

**In Checked Bag:**
- Main wardrobe
- Full-size toiletries
- Shoes (except what you're wearing)
- Guidebooks, maps
- Gifts or extra items

---

## Flight Details

### Outbound Flight

**See:** [Flights Research Page](../research/flights.qmd) for complete booking details

**Typical Schedule:**
- **Departure:** Chicago O'Hare (ORD) late afternoon/evening
- **Connection:** European hub (London, Munich, Zurich, etc.)
- **Arrival:** Pisa (PSA) morning of June 5

**Flight Duration:** ~12-14 hours total (including connection)

### Airport Departure (Madison or Chicago)

**Timing:**
- Arrive **3 hours before** international departure
- Check bags at airline counter
- TSA security screening
- Find departure gate
- Relax, stretch, eat, hydrate

**At the Airport:**
- Don't overeat (makes flight uncomfortable)
- Walk around to prepare for long sitting
- Fill water bottle after security
- Use bathroom right before boarding
- Charge devices at gate if needed

---

## In-Flight Strategy

### Transatlantic Leg

**For Sleep:**
- Set watch to Italy time immediately (6 hours ahead of Central Time)
- Try to sleep during "Italian night hours"
- Use eye mask, earplugs, neck pillow
- Decline meal if it interferes with sleep schedule
- Recline seat, get comfortable
- Hydrate but not excessively (bathroom trips interrupt sleep)

**For Comfort:**
- Walk the aisles every 2 hours (prevent blood clots)
- Stretch in seat when awake
- Compression socks if you have them
- Keep moving ankles/feet
- Hydrate throughout flight (avoid excessive alcohol)

**Entertainment:**
- Downloaded movies, podcasts, books
- Don't rely on in-flight entertainment (might not work)
- Noise-canceling headphones if you have them

### Connection in Europe

**At Hub Airport:**
- Follow signs for connecting flights
- **May need to clear customs/immigration** (depends on hub)
- Re-check departure boards for gate
- Short flights to Pisa (1-2 hours)
- Freshen up in bathroom
- Grab coffee if time allows
- Don't leave airport (stay airside)

---

## Arrival: June 5 Morning

### Landing in Pisa

**Arrival Time:** Likely 8-11 AM local time (depends on routing)

**After Landing:**
1. **Immigration** - Have passport ready, possibly fill out entry form
2. **Baggage Claim** - Collect checked bags
3. **Customs** - Usually just walk through "Nothing to Declare"
4. **Arrivals Hall** - You're in Italy!

**First Impressions:**
- You'll be tired (but excited!)
- Pisa airport is manageable, not overwhelming
- Follow signs in English and Italian
- Look for PisaMover shuttle to train station

### Next Steps

**See:** [Day 2 Itinerary](day-02-2026-06-05-pisa-to-florence.qmd) for arrival plans in Pisa and onward travel to Florence.

**Options:**
- **Option A:** Overnight in Pisa (rest after flight, see Leaning Tower, train to Florence next morning)
- **Option B:** Head straight to Florence (via PisaMover + train, ~1.5 hours total)

**We'll decide based on:** Flight arrival time, energy level, how tired we are.

---

## Important Numbers & Contacts

### Emergency Contacts (Save in Phone)

**U.S. Embassy in Rome:**
- Phone: +39 06 4674 1
- After-hours emergencies: +39 06 4674 2420

**Travel Insurance:**
- Policy number: [Fill in]
- Emergency contact: [Fill in]

**Airlines:**
- British Airways: 1-800-AIRWAYS
- Lufthansa: 1-800-645-3880
- [Your specific airline contact]

**Credit Card Lost/Stolen:**
- Visa: 1-800-847-2911
- Mastercard: 1-800-627-8372
- [Your specific card numbers]

### Home Contacts

- [Family member]: [Phone]
- [Friend with house key]: [Phone]
- [Work contact if needed]: [Phone]

---

## Travel Day Tips

### At Home Before Leaving

- Double-check passport is in carry-on
- Lock all doors and windows
- Set home security if applicable
- Final bathroom break
- Wear comfortable clothes and broken-in shoes for travel

### During Flight

**DO:**
- Stay hydrated (water, not just coffee/alcohol)
- Move around every 2 hours
- Set watch to destination time
- Try to sleep on Italy schedule
- Be kind to flight crew (they can make your trip better)

**DON'T:**
- Overdrink alcohol (dehydrates, causes jet lag)
- Stay seated entire flight (blood clot risk)
- Eat excessively (uncomfortable)
- Stress about connection times (airline will help if tight)
- Take sleeping pills without doctor approval

### Mental Preparation

**Remember:**
- This is an adventure, not a test
- Some discomfort is normal (long flights are tiring)
- You'll feel better after shower and sleep in Italy
- The journey is part of the experience
- Tomorrow you wake up in ITALY!

---

## Notes & Reflections

### Pre-Trip Thoughts

[Space to record feelings before departure]

**What I'm most excited about:**

**What I'm nervous about:**

**Goals for this trip:**

### Travel Day Observations

[Fill in during/after travel]

**Flight experience:**

**First impressions of Italy:**

**How I'm feeling:**

---

## Links & Resources

- [Flights Research](../research/flights.qmd) - Complete booking details
- [Packing List](../research/packing-list.qmd) - Final check
- [Day 2 Itinerary](day-02-2026-06-05-pisa-to-florence.qmd) - Arrival in Pisa

---

**Next Day:** [Day 2: Pisa to Florence](day-02-2026-06-05-pisa-to-florence.qmd)
